name: Sui Gu (Botu)
page_title: Sui Gu (Botu) 眭固 (白兔) [Black Mountain Bandits, Zhang Yang, Yuan Shao] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: Black Mountain Bandit Leader (One of Them)

sections.short-biography: Killed Yang Chou who had killed Zhang Yang. Turned to Yuan Shao but killed in battle.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	眭固 (白兔)


	 Simplified Chinese 
	眭固 (白兔)


	 Pronunciation 
	Sui1 Gu4 (Bai2tu4) 


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Suī Gù (Báitù)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Sui Ku (Poi-t‘u)

	 Birthplace 
	Unknown

	 Other Names 
	Gui Gu, Kui Gu

	 Name Notes 
	You will also find him referenced as Gui Gu and Kui Gu. In Moss Roberts’ translation he isn’t mentioned by name. ‘Kui Gu’ in the online Brewitt-Taylor translation. Koei, until recently, referenced him as Gui Gu. Bo 白 in his style is frequently translated as Bai, thus Baitu. Sidenote: his style, Botu (白兔) literally translates to ‘White Rabbit’.

sections.biography-internet-consistent:
Sui Gu, styled Botu (1), originally served under Zhang Yan, then under Zhang Yang. Shortly after Zhang Yang was killed by Yang Chou, Sui Gu killed Yang Chou to avenge the death of his lord. He then took the force and headed north to join Yuan Shao. In response, Cao Cao sent Shi Huan to attack him.

While Sui Gu was stationed at Shequan, a sorcerer told him “General, your style is Rabbit while the city’s name is Dog. When a rabbit sees a dog, it is naturally afraid. You ought to move elsewhere as soon as possible.” Sui Gu did not heed this advice.(2) Afterwards, he was defeated and executed by Shi Huan at Quancheng.

(1): Sui Gu’s zi is recorded in Dian Lüe (referenced by Pei Songzhi).

(2): The story of the sorcerer appears in Dian Lüe (referenced by Pei Songzhi). Sui Gu’s style means “White Rabbit”, Shequan means “Shooting (the) Dogs” and Quancheng means “Dog City”.

full_text:
Sui Gu (Botu) 眭固 (白兔)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sui Gu (眭固), styledBotu (白兔). Killed Yang Chou who had killed Zhang Yang. Turned to Yuan Shao but killed in battle. Sui Gu was a bandit affiliated with Zhang Yang and Yuan Shao. Sui Gu lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographyInternet (Consistent)
Sui Gu, styled Botu (1), originally served under Zhang Yan, then under Zhang Yang. Shortly after Zhang Yang was killed by Yang Chou, Sui Gu killed Yang Chou to avenge the death of his lord. He then took the force and headed north to join Yuan Shao. In response, Cao Cao sent Shi Huan to attack him.
While Sui Gu was stationed at Shequan, a sorcerer told him “General, your style is Rabbit while the city’s name is Dog. When a rabbit sees a dog, it is naturally afraid. You ought to move elsewhere as soon as possible.” Sui Gu did not heed this advice.(2) Afterwards, he was defeated and executed by Shi Huan at Quancheng.
(1): Sui Gu’sziis recorded inDian Lüe(referenced by Pei Songzhi).
(2): The story of the sorcerer appears inDian Lüe(referenced by Pei Songzhi). Sui Gu’s style means “White Rabbit”, Shequan means “Shooting (the) Dogs” and Quancheng means “Dog City”.
